title: Observability
summary: Three pillars of observability: metrics, logs, traces. OpenTelemetry, structured logging, distributed tracing, and SLOs.

sections.content:
## The Three Pillars

Observability is the ability to understand the internal state of a system from its external outputs. The three pillars provide complementary views:

- **Metrics** — Aggregated numbers over time. *What is happening?* (error rate, latency p99, CPU %)
- **Logs** — Discrete events with context. *What happened?* ("Order 123 failed: card declined")
- **Traces** — Request journeys across services. *Where did it happen?* (latency breakdown per service)

sections.title: Three Pillars of Observability
sections.content:
graph TD
    O[Observability]
    O --> M[Metrics\nCounters, Gauges, Histograms\nMicrometer → Prometheus → Grafana]
    O --> L[Logs\nStructured events\nLogback → Loki / ELK Stack]
    O --> T[Traces\nSpan trees across services\nOpenTelemetry → Jaeger / Tempo]
    M --> Alert[Alerting\nPagerDuty / OpsGenie]
    T --> Debug[Root cause analysis]
    L --> Debug
    style O fill:#4f46e5,color:#fff
    style Alert fill:#dc2626,color:#fff
    style Debug fill:#059669,color:#fff

## Metrics

Metrics are numerical measurements collected over time. Four key metric types:

| Type | Description | Example |
|---|---|---|
| **Counter** | Only goes up (reset on restart) | requests_total, errors_total |
| **Gauge** | Can go up or down | active_connections, memory_used |
| **Histogram** | Samples + sum + count (for percentiles) | request_duration_seconds |
| **Summary** | Calculated quantiles (expensive) | p50, p95, p99 latency |

```promql
# Prometheus queries
rate(http_requests_total[5m])              # requests per second
histogram_quantile(0.99, rate(request_duration_seconds_bucket[5m]))  # p99 latency
sum by (service) (rate(errors_total[5m]))  # errors by service
```

sections.title: OpenTelemetry Data Flow
sections.content:
graph LR
    App[Application\n+ OTel Agent] -->|OTLP| Collector[OTel Collector]
    Collector -->|metrics| Prom[Prometheus]
    Collector -->|traces| Jaeger[Jaeger / Tempo]
    Collector -->|logs| Loki[Loki]
    Prom --> Grafana[Grafana]
    Jaeger --> Grafana
    Loki --> Grafana
    style App fill:#4f46e5,color:#fff
    style Grafana fill:#059669,color:#fff

sections.question: Which observability pillar is best for answering 'Why is the checkout flow slow for some users?'
options: Metrics — look at p99 latency dashboards
Logs — search for slow checkout log entries
Traces — follow a specific request's journey through all services to find the slow span
Alerts — check if any SLO alerts fired
sections.explanation: Distributed traces show the complete journey of a specific request across all services with timing for each span. To diagnose why checkout is slow, you'd find a slow trace and examine which service/operation (span) is taking the most time. Metrics tell you that something is slow; traces tell you where.
